title: Getting Started with atspR
text: atspR (Automated Time-Series Preprocessing R) provides complete, modular preprocessing framework time-series data — raw input cross-validated, model-ready datasets.

title: Getting Started with atspR
code: devtools::install_github("example/Automated-Time-Series-Preprocessing-in-R")

title: Getting Started with atspR
code: library(atspR)  df <- data.frame(   date  = as.Date(c("2024-01-01","2024-01-02","2024-01-04","2024-01-05")),   sales = c(100, 120, NA, 130),   temp  = c(28.1, 27.5, 29.0, 28.8) )  # 1. Standardise missing values df <- standardize_na(df, na_strings = c("-", "Missing"))  # 2. Fill missing timestamps gap <- fill_time_gaps(df, time_col = "date", n = 1, unit = "day")  # 3. Run full pipeline result <- ts_preprocess(   data         = gap$data,   train_ratio  = 0.8,   scale_method = "minmax",   target_col   = "sales" )  result$cv_summary

title: Getting Started with atspR
code: df$datetime <- as.POSIXct(df$datetime,                            format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",                            tz     = "UTC")  gap    <- fill_time_gaps(df, time_col = "datetime", n = 1, unit = "hour") result <- ts_preprocess(data = gap$data, target_col = "temp")

what: Case 3: Date + Time in separate columns
title: Getting Started with atspR
code: # combine_datetime รองรับ time_type 3 แบบ: #   "hour"   → integer 0-23  เช่น 8, 14 #   "hhmm"   → integer HHMM  เช่น 830, 1430 #   "string" → character     เช่น "08:30", "14:30"  df2 <- combine_datetime(raw_data,                         date_col  = "Date",                         time_col  = "Time",                         new_col   = "datetime",                         time_type = "string",                         tz        = "Asia/Bangkok")  gap    <- fill_time_gaps(df2, time_col = "datetime", n = 1, unit = "hour") result <- ts_preprocess(   data          = gap$data,   impute_method = "knn",   scale_method  = "minmax",   target_col    = "VPD",   k_folds       = 5L )

title: Getting Started with atspR
text: atspR uses walk-forward validation — correct approach time-series. fold’s validation set always follows training set time.
code: Seed(20%)  Fold1  Fold2  Fold3  Fold4  Fold5 [─────────][─────][─────][─────][─────][─────]  Fold 1: train = seed              → val = fold1 Fold 2: train = seed + fold1      → val = fold2 Fold 3: train = seed + fold1+2    → val = fold3 data(airquality) clean <- airquality[complete.cases(airquality), ] sp    <- split_data(clean, verbose = FALSE) sc    <- scale_data(sp, method = "minmax", verbose = FALSE)  cv <- cross_validate(   sc,   target_col     = "Ozone",   k              = 5L,   min_train_size = 0.2 )  cv$summary cv$fold_results

title: Getting Started with atspR
code: export_pipeline(result, dir = "output", prefix = "atspR") # Saves: #   output/atspR_train_scaled.csv #   output/atspR_test_scaled.csv #   output/atspR_data_clean.csv #   output/atspR_missing_report.csv #   output/atspR_scale_params.csv #   output/atspR_cv_summary.csv

title: Authors and Citation
text: Data Scientist. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {atspR: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R},   author = {Data Scientist},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.0.0},   url = {https://github.com/example/Automated-Time-Series-Preprocessing-in-R}, }

title: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R
text: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing R

title: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R
text: atspR R package provides automated, modular preprocessing pipeline time-series data covering full journey raw sensor data cross-validated, model-ready train test sets. package designed students domain practitioners (agronomists, environmental scientists, irrigation engineers) work primarily R need reliable, leakage-free preprocessing workflow without manage multiple packages worry correct sequencing steps.

title: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R
code: Raw data    │    ├─ (Stage 1) combine_datetime()      Merge date + time columns → POSIXct (optional)    ├─ (Stage 2) fill_time_gaps()        Insert placeholder rows for missing timestamps    │    └─ ts_preprocess()                   ────────────────── 8 steps ──────────────────    │    (Stage 3) standardize_na()      Convert sentinel values to NA    │    (Stage 4) coerce_numeric()      Parse character columns to numeric    │    (Stage 5) split_data()          Temporal train/test split    │    (Stage 6) missing_analysis()    Summarise NAs; decide DROP or IMPUTE    │    (Stage 7) handle_missing()      Drop rows or interpolate (linear/KNN)    │    (Stage 8) visualize_data()      Scatter plots per variable vs time    │    (Stage 9) scale_data()          MinMax / Z-score / Robust (auto)    │    (Stage 10) cross_validate()     Walk-forward k-fold CV (optional)    │    └─ ts_export()                       Write all outputs to CSV

title: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R
code: # Install from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("EasternTechFusion/atspR")

what: Case 1: Date + Time in separate columns
title: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R
text: Sample data Hour 10:00 missing entirely — fill_time_gaps() insert row NA.
code: library(atspR)  df <- combine_datetime(my_data,                        date_col  = "Date",                        time_col  = "Time",                        new_col   = "datetime",                        time_type = "string")  gap <- fill_time_gaps(df,                        time_col = "datetime",                        n        = 1,                        unit     = "hour")  result <- ts_preprocess(data         = gap$data,                         train_ratio  = 0.8,                         target_col   = "VPD",                         k_folds      = 5)

title: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R
text: Sample data Hour 10:00 missing entirely — fill_time_gaps() insert row NA.
code: library(atspR)  df$datetime <- as.POSIXct(df$datetime,                            format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")                            gap <- fill_time_gaps(df,                        time_col = "datetime",                        n = 1,                        unit = "hour")                        result <- ts_preprocess(data         = gap$data,                         train_ratio  = 0.8,                         target_col   = "VPD",                         k_folds      = 5)

title: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R
text: Sample data 2024-01-03 missing entirely — fill_time_gaps() insert row NA.
code: library(atspR)  gap <- fill_time_gaps(df,                        time_col = "date",                        n = 1,                        unit = "day")                        result <- ts_preprocess(data         = gap$data,                         train_ratio  = 0.8,                         target_col   = "VPD",                         k_folds      = 5)

title: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R
code: ts_export(result, dir = "C:\Users\user\", prefix = "data")

title: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R
text: atspR uses walk-forward validation — correct approach time-series data. validation fold always preceded past data, future information leaks training.
code: Seed(20%)  Fold1  Fold2  Fold3  Fold4  Fold5 [─────────][─────][─────][─────][─────][─────]  Fold 1: train = seed              → val = fold1 Fold 2: train = seed + fold1      → val = fold2 Fold 3: train = seed + fold1+2    → val = fold3 ...

title: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R
code: Sueppong Mueanchamnong and Pattharaporn Thongnim (2026). atspR: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R. R package version 1.0.0 https://github.com/EasternTechFusion/atspR

title: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R
Auto-Convert Columns to Numeric — coerce_numeric
text: Scans character factor columns attempts convert numeric. column converted proportion values can parsed numbers meets min_success_rate threshold. Values parsed become NA.

title: Auto-Convert Columns to Numeric — coerce_numeric
code: coerce_numeric(data, cols = NULL, min_success_rate = 0.8, verbose = TRUE)

title: Auto-Convert Columns to Numeric — coerce_numeric
text: data data.frame. cols Character vector. Columns attempt conversion . NULL (default) tries character factor columns. min_success_rate Numeric (0, 1]. Minimum fraction non-NA values must parse successfully column converted. Default 0.8 (80%). verbose Logical (default TRUE).

title: Auto-Convert Columns to Numeric — coerce_numeric
text: data.frame eligible columns converted numeric.

title: Auto-Convert Columns to Numeric — coerce_numeric
text: step run standardize_na() custom missing indicators (e.g. "-", "Missing") already NA count success rate.

title: Auto-Convert Columns to Numeric — coerce_numeric
code: df <- data.frame(   date  = c("2024-01-01", "2024-01-02", "2024-01-03"),   temp  = c("25.1", "26.3", "NA"),   humid = c("80", "85", "90"),   label = c("A", "B", "C"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) # temp and humid will be converted; date and label will stay as character result <- coerce_numeric(df) #>  #> ============================================================ #>   COERCE TO NUMERIC #> ============================================================ #>  #>   Min success rate : 80% #>  #>  column success converted na_added #>    date      0%        no        3 #>    temp   66.7%        no        1 #>   humid    100%       YES        0 #>   label      0%        no        3 #>  #>   Converted : 1 column(s)  [humid] #>   Skipped   : 3 column(s)  [date, temp, label] #>  str(result) #> 'data.frame':	3 obs. of  4 variables: #>  $ date : chr  "2024-01-01" "2024-01-02" "2024-01-03" #>  $ temp : chr  "25.1" "26.3" "NA" #>  $ humid: num  80 85 90 #>  $ label: chr  "A" "B" "C"

what: Combine Separate Date and Time Columns into One Timestamp — combine_datetime
title: Combine Separate Date and Time Columns into One Timestamp — combine_datetime
text: Many datasets store date time separate columns. function merges single POSIXct column ready fill_time_gaps(). date format detected automatically.

title: Combine Separate Date and Time Columns into One Timestamp — combine_datetime
code: combine_datetime(   data,   date_col = "date",   time_col = "time",   new_col = "datetime",   date_format = NULL,   time_type = c("hour", "hhmm", "string"),   tz = "UTC",   drop_cols = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Combine Separate Date and Time Columns into One Timestamp — combine_datetime
text: data data.frame. date_col Character. Name date column. Supports many formats automatically: "2024-01-31" "2024/01/31" "31-01-2024" "31/01/2024" "01-31-2024" "01/31/2024" "31.01.2024" "2024.01.31" "31 Jan 2024" "January 31, 2024" "20240131" (compact) time_col Character. Name time/hour column. new_col Character. Name new combined column. Default "datetime". date_format Character. Override auto-detection strptime format string. Leave NULL (default) auto-detect. time_type Character. time column stored: "hour"   - integer 0-23, e.g. 8, 14 "hhmm"   - integer HHMM, e.g. 830, 1430 "string" - character "HH:MM" "HH:MM:SS" tz Character. Timezone. Default "UTC". drop_cols Logical. TRUE (default), remove date_col time_col combining. verbose Logical (default TRUE).

title: Combine Separate Date and Time Columns into One Timestamp — combine_datetime
text: original data.frame new POSIXct column prepended (optionally source columns removed).

title: Combine Separate Date and Time Columns into One Timestamp — combine_datetime
code: # Hour stored as integer df <- data.frame(   date = c("2024-01-01", "2024-01-01", "2024-01-01"),   hour = c(8L, 9L, 11L),   temp = c(25.1, 25.4, 26.2) ) df2 <- combine_datetime(df, date_col = "date", time_col = "hour",                         time_type = "hour") #>  #> ============================================================ #>   STEP : Combine Date + Time into One Column #> ============================================================ #>  #>   Date column : date  (format: %Y-%m-%d [auto]) #>   New column  : datetime  (tz: UTC) #>   Sample      : 2024-01-01 08:00:00 #>  #>   >> Next: fill_time_gaps() #>  df2$datetime #> [1] "2024-01-01 08:00:00 UTC" "2024-01-01 09:00:00 UTC" #> [3] "2024-01-01 11:00:00 UTC"  # Time stored as "HH:MM" string df3 <- data.frame(   date = c("01/01/2024", "01/01/2024"),   time = c("08:30", "09:00"),   val  = c(10, 12) ) df4 <- combine_datetime(df3, date_col = "date", time_col = "time",                         time_type = "string") #>  #> ============================================================ #>   STEP : Combine Date + Time into One Column #> ============================================================ #>  #>   Date column : date  (format: %Y/%m/%d [auto]) #>   New column  : datetime  (tz: UTC) #>   Sample      : 1-01-20 08:30:00 #>  #>   >> Next: fill_time_gaps() #>

what: K-Fold Cross-Validation on the Training Set — cross_validate
title: K-Fold Cross-Validation on the Training Set — cross_validate
text: Performs walk-forward (time-ordered) k-fold cross-validation using user-supplied model function. validation fold always preceded past data, future information leaks training.

title: K-Fold Cross-Validation on the Training Set — cross_validate
code: cross_validate(   scale_result = NULL,   train = NULL,   target_col,   model_fn = NULL,   k = 5L,   min_train_size = 0.2,   verbose = TRUE )

title: K-Fold Cross-Validation on the Training Set — cross_validate
text: scale_result list returned scale_data(); provides train_scaled.  Alternatively, supply plain data.frame train. train data.frame.  Used scale_result NULL. target_col Character.  Name response column. model_fn Function signature function(train_fold, val_fold). Must return named numeric vector / list includes least one performance metric (e.g. c(RMSE = ..., MAE = ..., R2 = ...)). simple linear-regression default provided NULL. k Integer.  Number folds (default 5). min_train_size Numeric (0, 1).  Fraction rows reserved seed training data folding begins.  Default 0.2 (20 %). ensures fold 1 always training data available. verbose Logical (default TRUE).

title: K-Fold Cross-Validation on the Training Set — cross_validate
text: invisible list elements: fold_results data.frame one row per fold. summary data.frame mean SD metric. k Number folds requested. k_evaluated Number folds actually evaluated. min_train_size Seed fraction used. target_col Response column name.

title: K-Fold Cross-Validation on the Training Set — cross_validate
text: seed training set size min_train_size reserved start data folding, guaranteeing every fold (including fold 1) enough data train .

title: K-Fold Cross-Validation on the Training Set — cross_validate

what: Fill Missing Timestamps in Time-Series Data — fill_time_gaps
title: Fill Missing Timestamps in Time-Series Data — fill_time_gaps
text: Handles two types missingness common time-series: Missing rows - timestamps absent entirely. rows inserted NA value columns. Missing values - rows exist NA columns. filling gaps result ready pass missing_analysis() handle_missing().

title: Fill Missing Timestamps in Time-Series Data — fill_time_gaps
code: fill_time_gaps(   data,   time_col,   n = 1,   unit = c("sec", "min", "hour", "day", "month", "quarter", "year"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Fill Missing Timestamps in Time-Series Data — fill_time_gaps
text: data data.frame, tibble, tsibble timestamp column, sorted ascending. tsibble tibble converted data.frame automatically. time_col Character. Name timestamp column (must Date POSIXct). Use combine_datetime() first date time stored separate columns. n Numeric. Number units per step. e.g. 1, 3, 15. unit Character. One "sec", "min", "hour", "day", "month", "quarter", "year". "month"   - calendar months (1, 2, 3, ...) "quarter" - calendar quarters 3 months (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) Note: "month" "quarter" require timestamp column Date value fall first day month (e.g. 2024-01-01, 2024-04-01). verbose Logical (default TRUE).

title: Fill Missing Timestamps in Time-Series Data — fill_time_gaps
text: invisible list elements: data Complete data.frame missing timestamps. Inserted rows NA value columns. n_gaps Number timestamp rows inserted. n_na_before Total NA cells gap-filling. n_na_after Total NA cells gap-filling (includes inserted rows). gap_timestamps Vector timestamps inserted. time_col Name timestamp column. freq Frequency string describing step used.

title: Fill Missing Timestamps in Time-Series Data — fill_time_gaps
text: tsibble tibble inputs automatically converted data.frame.

title: Fill Missing Timestamps in Time-Series Data — fill_time_gaps
Handle Missing Values — handle_missing
text: Automatically chooses imputation row deletion based two criteria: dataset size (n) overall missing rate.

title: Handle Missing Values — handle_missing
code: handle_missing(   analysis_result = NULL,   data = NULL,   method = c("linear", "knn"),   knn_k = 5L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Handle Missing Values — handle_missing
text: analysis_result list returned missing_analysis(). Alternatively, pass plain data.frame data. data data.frame.  Used analysis_result NULL. method Character.  Imputation method: "linear" (default) "knn". knn_k Integer.  Number neighbours KNN (default 5). verbose Logical.  Print progress messages (default TRUE).

title: Handle Missing Values — handle_missing
text: invisible list elements: data_clean cleaned data.frame. action Character: "drop" "impute". method Imputation method used ("none"). imputed_mask Logical data.frame marking imputed cells (dimensions data_clean).  FALSE action "drop". n_imputed Integer.  Number cells imputed. report short character string summarising action taken.

title: Handle Missing Values — handle_missing
text: Decision rules: n <= 50                      -> always impute (data small lose rows) n > 50 missing > 5%   -> impute n > 50 missing <= 5%  -> drop rows NA imputing, linear interpolation default; KNN imputation available alternative.

title: Handle Missing Values — handle_missing
Inverse-Transform Scaled Data — inverse_scale
text: Reverses scaling applied scale_data() recover original units.

title: Inverse-Transform Scaled Data — inverse_scale
code: inverse_scale(scaled_data, scale_result)

title: Inverse-Transform Scaled Data — inverse_scale
text: scaled_data data.frame scaled values. scale_result list returned scale_data().

title: Inverse-Transform Scaled Data — inverse_scale
text: data.frame scaled columns back original units.

title: Inverse-Transform Scaled Data — inverse_scale
code: data(airquality) clean <- airquality[complete.cases(airquality), ] sp    <- split_data(clean, verbose = FALSE) sc    <- scale_data(sp, verbose = FALSE) original_train <- inverse_scale(sc$train_scaled, sc)

what: Analyse Missing Values in a Time-Series Dataset — missing_analysis
title: Analyse Missing Values in a Time-Series Dataset — missing_analysis
text: Computes per-variable missing-value summary table draws boxplots every numeric column. Results returned invisibly list can passed directly handle_missing().

title: Analyse Missing Values in a Time-Series Dataset — missing_analysis
code: missing_analysis(data, plot = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)

title: Analyse Missing Values in a Time-Series Dataset — missing_analysis
text: data data.frame tibble. plot Logical. TRUE (default) boxplots drawn. verbose Logical. TRUE (default) summary table printed.

title: Analyse Missing Values in a Time-Series Dataset — missing_analysis
text: invisible list elements: summary data.frame columns variable, n_missing, pct_missing, pct_missing_num. overall_pct Overall fraction missing cells (numeric 0-1). plot ggplot object (boxplots), NULL. data original data passed (unchanged).

title: Analyse Missing Values in a Time-Series Dataset — missing_analysis
code: data(airquality) result <- missing_analysis(airquality) #>  #> ============================================================ #>   STEP 3/7 : Missing Value Analysis #> ============================================================ #>  #>   Rows    : 153  |  Cols: 6  |  Total cells: 918 #>   Missing : 4.79%  (44 cells)  |  2 / 6 cols affected #>   Action  : DROP  -- missing = 4.79% <= 5% threshold, safe to remove rows #>  #> ------------------------------------------------------------ #>   Missing per column #> ------------------------------------------------------------ #>  variable n_missing pct_missing #>     Ozone        37      24.18% #>   Solar.R         7       4.58% #>    result$summary #>         variable n_missing pct_missing_num pct_missing #> Ozone      Ozone        37      0.24183007      24.18% #> Solar.R  Solar.R         7      0.04575163       4.58% #> Wind        Wind         0      0.00000000          0% #> Temp        Temp         0      0.00000000          0% #> Month      Month         0      0.00000000          0% #> Day          Day         0      0.00000000          0%

title: Scale Numeric Features (Auto-Select Method) — scale_data
text: Fits scaler training set applies parameters train test sets, preventing data leakage.

title: Scale Numeric Features (Auto-Select Method) — scale_data
code: scale_data(split_result, method = "auto", cols = NULL, verbose = TRUE)

title: Scale Numeric Features (Auto-Select Method) — scale_data
text: split_result list returned split_data(). method Character. One "auto" (default), "minmax", "zscore", "robust". Use "auto" let function choose. cols Character vector. Names numeric columns scale. NULL (default) scales numeric columns. verbose Logical (default TRUE).

title: Scale Numeric Features (Auto-Select Method) — scale_data
text: invisible list elements: train_scaled Scaled training data.frame. test_scaled Scaled test data.frame. params Per-column scaling parameters (fitted train ). method scaling method used. method_reason method selected (auto mode ). outlier_ratio Named numeric vector outlier ratios per column. cols Character vector scaled column names.

title: Scale Numeric Features (Auto-Select Method) — scale_data
text: method = "auto" (default), scaling method chosen automatically based per-column outlier detection (IQR method) normality test (Shapiro-Wilk): robust  – outlier found column (IQR fence) zscore  – outliers + majority columns approximately normal minmax  – outliers + majority columns normal

title: Scale Numeric Features (Auto-Select Method) — scale_data
text: minmax Scales feature \([0, 1]\). zscore Standardises zero mean unit variance. robust Uses median IQR, robust outliers.

title: Scale Numeric Features (Auto-Select Method) — scale_data
code: data(airquality) clean <- airquality[complete.cases(airquality), ] sp    <- split_data(clean, verbose = FALSE) sc    <- scale_data(sp) #>  #> ============================================================ #>   STEP 7/7 : Feature Scaling  [ROBUST] #> ============================================================ #>  #>   Auto-selected : outlier detected in 2 column(s) [Ozone, Wind] -> robust #>   Columns : 6  (Ozone, Solar.R, Wind, ...) #>   Fitted on TRAIN only  |  Columns with outliers: 2 / 6 #>  #> ------------------------------------------------------------ #>   Outlier ratio per column  (IQR) #> ------------------------------------------------------------ #>  column outlier_pct #>    Wind       3.41% #>   Ozone       1.14% #>  #>   [OK] Outliers found in [Ozone, Wind] -> robust scaling applied #>  sc$method #> [1] "robust" sc$method_reason #> [1] "outlier detected in 2 column(s) [Ozone, Wind] -> robust"

what: Split Data into Temporal Train and Test Sets — split_data
title: Split Data into Temporal Train and Test Sets — split_data
text: Performs time-ordered (non-random) train/test split training set always precedes test set chronologically – correct approach time-series dataset row order encodes time.

title: Split Data into Temporal Train and Test Sets — split_data
code: split_data(data, train_ratio = 0.8, verbose = TRUE)

title: Split Data into Temporal Train and Test Sets — split_data
text: data data.frame (rows assumed ordered time). train_ratio Numeric (0, 1).  Fraction rows training. Default 0.8 (80 / 20 split). verbose Logical (default TRUE).

title: Split Data into Temporal Train and Test Sets — split_data
text: invisible list elements: train Training data.frame. test Test data.frame. train_idx Integer vector training row indices. test_idx Integer vector test row indices. ratio Named numeric c(train = ..., test = ...).

title: Split Data into Temporal Train and Test Sets — split_data
code: data(airquality) clean <- airquality[complete.cases(airquality), ] sp    <- split_data(clean, train_ratio = 0.7) #>  #> ============================================================ #>   TRAIN / TEST SPLIT  (temporal) #> ============================================================ #>  #>   Total rows  : 111 #>   Train ratio : 69%  (77 rows) #>   Test  ratio : 31%  (34 rows) #>  nrow(sp$train); nrow(sp$test) #> [1] 77 #> [1] 34

what: Standardise Missing Value Indicators to NA — standardize_na
title: Standardise Missing Value Indicators to NA — standardize_na
text: Converts custom "missing" representations "-", "Missing", "N/", "none", "null", "", user-defined string/number proper NA values rest pipeline can detect handle correctly.

title: Standardise Missing Value Indicators to NA — standardize_na
code: standardize_na(   data,   na_strings = character(0),   na_numbers = NULL,   cols = NULL,   trim = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Standardise Missing Value Indicators to NA — standardize_na
text: data data.frame. na_strings Character vector. Additional strings treat NA. combined built-defaults. Set character(0) use defaults. na_numbers Numeric vector. Numeric sentinel values treat NA, e.g. -999, 9999, 0. Default NULL (none). cols Character vector. Column names process. NULL (default) processes columns. trim Logical. TRUE (default), trim leading/trailing whitespace character columns matching. verbose Logical (default TRUE).

title: Standardise Missing Value Indicators to NA — standardize_na
text: data.frame matched values replaced NA.

title: Standardise Missing Value Indicators to NA — standardize_na

what: Export Preprocessing Results to CSV — ts_export
title: Export Preprocessing Results to CSV — ts_export
text: Saves scaled train test sets (optionally cleaned data summary report) produced ts_preprocess() CSV files specified directory.

title: Export Preprocessing Results to CSV — ts_export
code: ts_export(   result,   dir = "atspR_output",   prefix = "atspR",   export_clean = TRUE,   export_report = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Export Preprocessing Results to CSV — ts_export
text: result list returned ts_preprocess(). dir Character. Directory save files. Created exist. Default "atspR_output". prefix Character. Filename prefix. Default "atspR". export_clean Logical. Also export data_clean. Default TRUE. export_report Logical. Also export missing_report scale_params CSV. Default TRUE. verbose Logical (default TRUE).

title: Export Preprocessing Results to CSV — ts_export
text: Invisibly returns named character vector exported file paths.

title: Export Preprocessing Results to CSV — ts_export
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(airquality) result <- ts_preprocess(airquality, verbose = FALSE) ts_export(result, dir = "output", prefix = "airquality") } # }

what: Run the Full Preprocessing Pipeline — ts_preprocess
title: Run the Full Preprocessing Pipeline — ts_preprocess
text: Executes pipeline steps order: standardize_na()  - convert custom missing indicators NA coerce_numeric()  - auto-convert character cols numeric fill_time_gaps()  - insert missing timestamps (optional) split_data()      - temporal train/test split (imputation) missing_analysis() - assess missing values train handle_missing()   - impute/drop train; forward-fill test train tail visualize_data()   - scatter plots per variable vs index scale_data()       - feature scaling (fit train ) cross_validate()   - k-fold CV training set (optional)

title: Run the Full Preprocessing Pipeline — ts_preprocess
code: ts_preprocess(   data,   na_strings = character(0),   na_numbers = NULL,   min_success_rate = 0.8,   train_ratio = 0.8,   impute_method = "linear",   impute_tail_n = 10L,   scale_method = "auto",   target_col = NULL,   k_folds = 5L,   model_fn = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run the Full Preprocessing Pipeline — ts_preprocess
text: data data.frame rows ordered time. na_strings Character vector. Extra strings treat NA, e.g. c("-", "Missing", "N/"). Built-defaults already cover common patterns. Default character(0). na_numbers Numeric vector. Numeric sentinel values treat NA, e.g. c(-999, 9999). Default NULL. min_success_rate Numeric (0, 1]. Minimum fraction non-NA values must parse numbers character column converted numeric. Default 0.8. train_ratio Numeric (0, 1). Default 0.8. impute_method "linear" (default) "knn". scale_method "auto" (default) auto-selects based outlier detection, one "minmax", "zscore", "robust". target_col Character. Response column cross-validation. NULL (default), CV skipped. k_folds Integer. Number CV folds. Default 5. model_fn Optional custom model function CV. verbose Logical (default TRUE).

title: Run the Full Preprocessing Pipeline — ts_preprocess
text: invisible list elements: data_clean Cleaned data.frame. train Unscaled training set. test Unscaled test set. train_scaled Scaled training set – ready modelling. test_scaled Scaled test set. scale_params Scaling parameters (fitted train ). scale_method Scaling method used. missing_report Per-variable NA summary table. imputation_report String describing action taken. cv_summary CV summary data.frame, NULL skipped. cv_folds Per-fold CV results, NULL skipped. plots Named list: boxplot, scatter. before_after /imputation sample, NULL.

title: Run the Full Preprocessing Pipeline — ts_preprocess
code: data(airquality)  # Basic usage result <- ts_preprocess(   data         = airquality,   train_ratio  = 0.8,   scale_method = "minmax" ) #>  #> ============================================================ #>   atspR  |  Automated Time Series Preprocessing #> ============================================================ #>  #>   Input : 153 rows, 6 cols  |  Train/Test: 80%/20%  |  Impute: linear  |  Scale: minmax #>   CV    : target = none (skipped),  k = 5 #> ------------------------------------------------------------   #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the atspR package. #>   Please report the issue at #>   <https://github.com/example/Automated-Time-Series-Preprocessing-in-R/issues>.   #>   [1/8] Standardise NA indicators  -- done #>  #>   [2/8] Coerce character cols to numeric  -- done #>  #> (LINEAR)  -- missing = 5.87% > 5% threshold #>  #>   [5/8] Handle Missing Values  (train) #>         43 cells imputed  [Ozone:36  Solar.R:7] #>  #>   [6/8] Handle Missing Values  (test -- forward-fill from train tail) #>         1 NA cells filled  |  tail_n = 10 rows used #>  #>   [7/8] Visualise #>         2 scatter page(s) generated #>  #>   [8/8] Feature Scaling  [MINMAX] #>         Columns what: Visualise Data After Missing-Value Handling — visualize_data
title: Visualise Data After Missing-Value Handling — visualize_data
text: Produces scatter plots numeric column vs row index (time order). 4 subplots shown per page – 4 numeric columns, multiple plots printed sequentially. imputation performed, imputed points highlighted red.

title: Visualise Data After Missing-Value Handling — visualize_data
code: visualize_data(   handle_result,   raw_data = NULL,   cols_per_page = 4L,   n_sample = 10L,   verbose = TRUE,   time_col = NULL,   x_col = NULL,   y_col = NULL )

title: Visualise Data After Missing-Value Handling — visualize_data
text: handle_result list returned handle_missing(). raw_data data.frame. original (pre-cleaning) dataset. Required handle_result$action == "impute". cols_per_page Integer. Maximum subplots per page. Default 4. n_sample Integer. Rows show /console table. Default 10. verbose Logical (default TRUE). time_col Character. Name datetime column use x-axis. NULL (default), row index used instead. x_col, y_col Ignored. Kept backward compatibility.

title: Visualise Data After Missing-Value Handling — visualize_data
text: invisible list elements scatter_plots (list ggplot objects, one per page) before_after (data.frame NULL).

title: Visualise Data After Missing-Value Handling — visualize_data
code: data(airquality) ana   <- missing_analysis(airquality, plot = FALSE, verbose = FALSE) clean <- handle_missing(ana, verbose = FALSE) viz   <- visualize_data(clean, raw_data = airquality)  #>   [Plot 1/2] columns: Ozone, Solar.R, Wind, Temp  #>   [Plot 2/2] columns: Month, Day #>
